description: Address semantic review findings: persistent connections in SQLite stores, fix redundant prompt construction, add schema initialization guard, restore _tick from DB, handle LLM error responses in eval runner.
steps: Fix persistence stores (sqlite_store.py, sqlite_concept_registry.py, sqlite_theory_registry.py, sqlite_identity_store.py) to use a persistent connection per instance with close() method
Add module-level set in migrations.py to track which db_paths have been initialized, skipping redundant DDL
In SQLiteMemoryStore.__init__, restore _tick from SELECT MAX(last_accessed) FROM memories
Fix run_single_condition in run_real_llm_eval.py to call _call_openrouter directly with the locally-built prompt
In run_single_condition, check if response starts with [LLM_ERROR] and short-circuit verification
Run all tests to verify nothing is broken
acceptance_criteria: Each SQLite store opens one connection in __init__ and reuses it
Each SQLite store has a close() method
initialize_database only runs DDL once per db_path per process
SQLiteMemoryStore restores _tick from max(last_accessed) on construction
run_single_condition uses the locally-built prompt directly via _call_openrouter
LLM error responses are detected and short-circuit verification with error field
findings: All 408 tests pass. Persistent connections eliminate repeated connect/close cycles. The _initialized_paths set avoids DDL re-execution. _tick restoration ensures decay semantics are correct across restarts. The eval runner now sends the correct condition-specific prompt directly via _call_openrouter. LLM errors are detected early and bypass verification.
